requiredChanges.issue: Step says action='create' for tests/visual/systems/wireframePolyhedra.test.ts but that file already exists with 40 tests (T-054-01 through T-054-40). Action should be 'edit' to add new test cases for aEdgeId attribute and positions getter.
requiredChanges.fix: Change action to 'edit' for wireframePolyhedra.test.ts. Only polyhedraEdges.test.ts is a new file (action='create').

requiredChanges.issue: Description says 'Add tests for the generator and system' as if starting from scratch. The system test file already has comprehensive coverage of mesh creation, attribute binding, uniform updates, cleanup, setOpacity, determinism, volumetric placement, fog, privacy, and boundary values. New tests should only cover the delta: aEdgeId attribute presence/itemSize, positions getter returning Float32Array, and generator edgeIds field.
requiredChanges.fix: Scope system tests to: (1) aEdgeId attribute exists with itemSize 1, (2) positions getter returns non-empty Float32Array, (3) positions getter length matches expected vertex count. Generator tests for polyhedraEdges.test.ts are new and correctly scoped.

requiredChanges.issue: shaderRegistry.test.ts also already exists with 4 tests (T-054-45 through T-054-48) and is not mentioned in step 5, but will need a new test case asserting aEdgeId is present in WIREPOLYHEDRA_ATTRIBUTES.
requiredChanges.fix: Add shaderRegistry.test.ts to the files list in step 5 (action='edit') with a test verifying aEdgeId with itemSize 1 is in WIREPOLYHEDRA_ATTRIBUTES.

suggestions.note: Acceptance criterion 'edgeIds length equals positions length / 3' is correct (one float per vertex, positions has 3 floats per vertex) but the wording is confusing. Consider 'edgeIds.length equals vertexCount (positions.length / 3)' for clarity.

The positions getter will need to apply mesh.position (world offset) to each vertex since each polyhedron is placed at a random position via mesh.position.set(). Ensure the description clarifies that localToWorld or manual offset addition is needed, not just reading raw buffer data.

The fragment shader hue offset '+ 0.05 * (vEdgeId - 0.5)' produces a range of ±0.025 on the 0–1 hue scale (±9 degrees). This is reasonable but consider that the hue is already mod(uPaletteHue, 360.0) / 360.0, so the offset is applied post-normalization — confirm this doesn't cause hue wrapping artifacts near 0/1 boundary.

planAccuracy.systemClaims: Verified: no positions getter, no aEdgeId attribute binding. Factory, modeManager, and main.ts integration all confirmed at described locations

planAccuracy.testClaims: INACCURATE: wireframePolyhedra.test.ts and shaderRegistry.test.ts already exist with comprehensive coverage. Only polyhedraEdges.test.ts is truly new
